chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The topic is graph coloring problem. This can be solved using backtracking.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: First of all let us know what the problem is. See a graph is given and some colors are given.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us say three colors are given that is red, green and blue.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now the problem is we have to color the vertices of a graph such that

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: no two neighboring vertices or adjacent vertices must have same color.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So three colors are given to me.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us try and see what colors we can give whether it is possible or not possible.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I'll start from vertex 1. I'll give red color to this one.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now I'll pick up second vertex 2.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Who are neighboring to 2, 1 and 3 but already red color is there to this neighboring vertex.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this 2 cannot take red color. So okay, we'll give green color.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now go to vertex 3. Which color I can give? Can I give red color? It's not adjacent to 1.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes, I can take red color.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then vertex 4. Can I give red color? This is adjacent to this one. No. Can I give green color?

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So is it adjacent? Is it adjacent? No, I can't take this color.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then here can I give red color? No, this is adjacent to this one. Can I give green color?

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is adjacent to this so I cannot take green color. Can I take blue color? Yes, this is not green.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is not so it's different from all three so I can take blue color.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So I have vertices 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And the colors what I have now is red, green, red,

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: green and blue.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes, a graph can be colored like this.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Is it the only solution or there are other solutions possible?

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Yes, I can color it in a different way also. Let us see instead of red color. I'll give green color here.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And here I cannot take green. So I'll take red here and here I cannot take red. So I'll take green.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And instead of green I'll give here red color and this is blue. Okay, I'll give blue only for this one.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See for the other vertices the colors has changed.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It means that same vertices can be colored in different ways. So there are more than one solutions possible.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And here we want all possible solution. What are the ways they can be colored?

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it is like permutation or trying out all possibilities and then picking up the one which are satisfying the condition.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: What is the condition?

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Neighboring vertices should not have same color. So yes, this type of problem can be solved using

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: backtracking. When you want multiple solutions and there is a different possible solution go for backtracking.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here we learn two things. See three colors were given to us and we have colored the graph.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I give just two colors, I don't give blue color.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Can the graph be colored in this one?

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: No, it cannot be colored.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if I give four colors one more color yellow, can the graph be colored in that? Yes, it can be colored.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So we will define one type of problem here.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If a graph is given and some colors are also given

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and just we want to know whether a graph can be colored by using those colors or not.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We don't want the colors. We don't want the colors of the vertices. Just we want to know it can be colored or not.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this problem is called as M coloring decision problem. It is M coloring

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: M coloring decision problem.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We'll define one more problem here. If a graph is given and we want to know

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: minimum how many colors required for coloring the graph and this problem is called as M coloring optimization problem.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: M coloring

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Optimization problem.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now we can say there are three type of problems. One is if a graph is given and the colors are given

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: we want to know in how many ways the

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: vertices can be colored. What are the possible colors? This is one type of problem.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Second thing is colors are given just we want to know whether the graph can be colored with those vertices or not.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And the third thing is colors are not given just we want to know minimum how many colors are required for coloring this graph.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So before start coloring first of all, we need to know minimum how many colors we should have. So for that we go for

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: optimization problem.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Or else we already have some colors and we want to know whether these colors will be sufficient to

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: color this graph or not. So then this is a problem

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: M coloring decision problem. This is also called as chromatic number problem.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Chromatic number problem. M coloring is chromatic number. So decision problem or optimization problem.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now we'll take a simple graph and show you

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: how this can be solved using backtracking. Here is a simple graph and three colors are given.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have to color those vertices such that no two adjacent vertices are having same color.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this problem can be solved using backtracking.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And the method of solving a problem in backtracking is by generating a state space tree.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: First I will generate the state space tree without any conditions or any checkings.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I will simply generate a state space tree.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us take this is the root vertex then first I will consider the first vertex, vertex 1.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is for vertex 1. I can call it as x1. For this I can give either red color or green color or blue color.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And second vertex, second level second vertex can have

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: either red color or green color or blue color. Then here also

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: red color, green

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: or blue,

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: red,

chunks.text: green

chunks.text: Then under this, this is for the third vertex.

chunks.text: Third vertex red color

chunks.text: So I have filled all possibilities for third vertex now, I will fill for fourth vertex.

chunks.text: Now this tree is showing all possibilities in which I can color them without checking the adjacency condition.

chunks.text: Without checking any condition means the neighboring vertices can have same color also.

chunks.text: So I will fill for the fourth vertex.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Without checking any condition means the neighboring vertices can have same color also.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is the possibility. I have drawn a big graph. It becomes very big if you don't apply any condition.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But in backtracking we impose conditions and solve the problem.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But without any conditions if I generate let us check total how many nodes are generated.

chunks.text: That is root is having first node then

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: first level is having three nodes.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The second level is having three into three nodes.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then third level is having three into three into three nodes that is 27 nodes.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 27 and the next level is having three into three into three into three nodes.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See these many nodes are generated without imposing any bounding function or condition. Then how many nodes are these?

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 1 plus 3 plus 3 square plus 3 cube plus 3 power 4.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For four vertices it is forming 3 power 4.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 3 to the power of 4 plus 1 minus 1 by 3 minus 1.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is 3 power 4 plus 1 minus 1 by 2.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is approximately 3 power n plus 1. n is the number of vertices and what is 3 here?

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: c, c to the power of n plus 1.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The time complexity for generating these means time is nothing but the number of nodes you are generating for each node

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: you consider this one unit of time.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Total time span is

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 3 power n or c power n. This is exponential time taking problem.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now, let me solve it using backtracking.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We imagine that all these nodes are generated but backtracking will reduce it.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Still we consider the time is this much only.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I will solve this using backtracking by also checking bounding function. Let us start. I will generate a state space tree.

chunks.text: So we say that the time complexity for solving this is 3 power n. Now, let me show you where the graph coloring problem is useful.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now, let us see where this graph coloring problem is useful. So here I have a map and the map is having different regions.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose I have to color this map such that no two adjacent regions are having same color.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Why I want to know this? See the reason is in olden days when the printing was done

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: for each color

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the paper has to be passed through the printing press for one time.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So for n colors, it should be passed for n times.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if we say let us take unique color for each region then that many times we have to pass the paper through a

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: printing press. To avoid this extra work

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: we want a solution so that in minimum number of colors this map can be colored.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that minimum number of times it can go through and the cost of printing is reduced.

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that's how

chunks.text: this problem is introduced. So the map can be converted into a

chunks.text: graph and the graph can be solved. So we get a solution for a

chunks.title: Graph Coloring Problem - Backtracking - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: map.

text: The topic is graph coloring problem. This can be solved using backtracking. First of all let us know what the problem is. See a graph is given and some colors are given. Let us say three colors are given that is red, green and blue. Now the problem is we have to color the vertices of a graph such that no two neighboring vertices or adjacent vertices must have same color. So three colors are given to me. Let us try and see what colors we can give whether it is possible or not possible. So I'll start from vertex 1. I'll give red color to this one. Now I'll pick up second vertex 2. Who are neighboring to 2, 1 and 3 but already red color is there to this neighboring vertex. So this 2 cannot take red color. So okay, we'll give green color. Now go to vertex 3. Which color I can give? Can I give red color? It's not adjacent to 1. Yes, I can take red color. Then vertex 4. Can I give red color? This is adjacent to this one. No. Can I give green color? So is it adjacent? Is it adjacent? No, I can't take this color. Then here can I give red color? No, this is adjacent to this one. Can I give green color? This is adjacent to this so I cannot take green color. Can I take blue color? Yes, this is not green. This is not so it's different from all three so I can take blue color. So I have vertices 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5. And the colors what I have now is red, green, red, green and blue. Yes, a graph can be colored like this.
